claim: Lovable and bolt.new perform better at creating initial project foundations because they optimize for a single LLM, while bolt.DIY works best for iterative improvements with free models.
actionable: Use lovable or bolt.new to establish your initial frontend foundation with high quality, then migrate to bolt.DIY for cost-free iterations and tweaks.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps

claim: Combining multiple AI coding assistants (lovable for foundation, bolt.DIY for iteration, windsurf/cursor for polish) creates a workflow that balances power, cost, and development speed.
actionable: Build your full-stack AI apps by starting with lovable, iterating extensively in bolt.DIY, then bringing into windsurf/cursor for final touches.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps

claim: You should specify the input/output schema, API endpoint details, and message handling logic explicitly in prompts to AI coding assistants, not leave them to infer it.
actionable: When prompting AI coding assistants to build frontends, include explicit API schema, endpoint URL, input/output structures, and how messages are handled.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps

claim: Supabase authentication setup with email and password requires just adding a single line to your prompt to AI coding assistants; no additional backend configuration is needed.
actionable: Add 'enable superbase authentication with email and password' to your AI assistant prompt to automatically set up user auth without manual config.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps

claim: Real-time subscriptions to database tables allow frontends to immediately pull new messages from agents; this should be explicitly specified in prompts so coding assistants set up the subscription correctly.
actionable: Specify that your frontend needs real-time subscriptions to database changes when prompting AI coding assistants, so they implement Supabase real-time correctly.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps

claim: bolt.DIY has a web container that blocks requests to localhost agents, so you can't fully test agent communication within bolt.DIY; you must download and run locally to test API calls.
actionable: Don't expect to test your agent API calls within bolt.DIY; download the project and run locally to verify that your frontend correctly communicates with your agent.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps

claim: Request one feature or change at a time when iterating with AI coding assistants; this makes it much easier for the LLM to understand and implement correctly compared to batch requests.
actionable: Make incremental, one-change-at-a-time prompts to your AI coding assistant rather than batch requests to get more accurate implementations.
source_title: INSANE New Strategy for Building AI Agent Apps
